element_introduction_order.mastery_context: whip swing across pits used throughout
breaks mold from typical action platforming

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: challenging with frequent combat and platforming demands; broken by occasional driving/flying/rafting sequences that shift gameplay mode

difficulty_curve.early_game: Raiders of the Lost Ark acts as primary entry point; reviews cite varying difficulty assessment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Temple of Doom unlocked via progression or password; vehicle sequences provide pacing relief
difficulty_curve.late_game: Last Crusade final challenge; includes skeleton combat and complex platforming
notable_spikes: difficulty spikes noted by EGM reviewers with frequent unavoidable hits
described as 'brutally hard' and 'extremely challenging' by multiple sources
contrasts with GamePro assessment of stages being 'far too easy'

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divided across three Indiana Jones films with separate progressions; Raiders playable at start, Temple of Doom and Last Crusade gated behind progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Raiders of the Lost Ark (film 1) → Temple of Doom (film 2) → Last Crusade (film 3)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mix of side-view platforming and alternative vehicle/driving stages providing gameplay variety
notable_level_patterns.example_level: plane flying, mine cart, raft sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Death restarts from area beginning or last checkpoint rather than level start; reduces frustration from long sections
Whip serves dual purpose: primary melee weapon and swing mechanism for pit traversal
